title: Predictions for 2026: the post-SaaS reality of enterprise AI | by Jens Eriksvik | Medium
full_text: # Predictions for 2026: the post-SaaS reality of enterprise AI. **It is that time of year again. **I like to think that this set of predictions comes from a different place (!). They are a set of learnings from two years of agent pilots, pricing experiments, honest mistakes, risk management, and architectural workarounds. * SaaS platforms are not the long-term hosts for agents. When agent logic lives inside vendor platforms, enterprises lose control over behavior, cost, governance, and accountability. SaaS systems remain essential, but as tools and systems of record, not autonomous actors. The net conclusion is not that AI agents fail. AI succeeds when autonomy is constrained, execution is owned, and determinism is treated as an asset rather than a limitation. ## **Prediction 1: 2026 closes the chapter on “AI magic” in enterprise SaaS**. ## **Prediction 4: AI agents move out of SaaS and into enterprise-owned platforms**. It is what locks in the move to enterprise-owned agent platforms. / score=0.300 / query=Applied AI architect enterprise customer workflow / intent=find_customer_case / path=industry_landing

They are a set of learnings from two years of agent pilots, pricing experiments, honest mistakes, risk management, and architectural workarounds. * SaaS platforms are not the long-term hosts for agents. When agent logic lives inside vendor platforms, enterprises lose control over behavior, cost, governance, and accountability. SaaS systems remain essential, but as tools and systems of record, not autonomous actors. The net conclusion is not that AI agents fail.
